content:
Consider a hash table of size 10 with indices $\{0,1, \ldots, 9\}$, with the hash function

$$ h(x)=3 x(\bmod 10) $$

where linear probing is used to handle collisions. The hash table is initially empty and then the following sequence of keys is inserted into the hash table: 1 , $4,5,6,14,15$. The indices where the keys 14 and 15 are stored are, respectively
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & 1 \rightarrow 3 \\ & 4 \rightarrow 2 \\ & 5 \rightarrow 5 \\ & 6 \rightarrow 8 \\ & 14 \rightarrow 4 \\ & 15 \rightarrow 6 \end{aligned} $$

As 5 already filled.

(As 2, 3 already occupied)

$$ h(x)=3 \times(\bmod 10) $$

15 is inserted to location 6 as location 5 is filled. So, 14 is started to location 4 and 15 is stored to location 6.

$44 \cot \times \cos t$

57 bnf $\times$ Bcnf

64 shite $\times$ White

65 adjacency lis

Hence, the correct option is (A).
